q: Which property of a medium determines the rate at which sound is attenuated?
In a liquid medium, the velocity of sound is given by ___.
Longitudinal sound waves travel fastest in ___.
The SI units for Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are ___.
The threshold of hearing corresponds to a minimum intensity of sound of ___.
___ is the unit used for measuring sound intensity level.
If the sound intensity is increased by a factor of 1000, the value of sound intensity increases by ___.
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the ___ reaching an eardrum.
An echo is a repetition of sound due to the ___ of sound.
Which material is used in buildings to absorb incident sound and reduce echoes?
The formula used to determine distance $d$ between a source and reflecting surface using echo time $t$ is ___.
The special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the echoes is called ___.
The range of frequencies a person can hear is known as the range of ___.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit of audibility is ___.
___ technology works based on echoes and is used for navigation at sea.
Infrasound waves are waves with frequencies ___.
Which animal can communicate over long distances using frequencies as low as $15 Hz$?
The change in frequency of a wave related to an observer due to relative motion is the ___.
Doppler effect in electromagnetic radiations used to determine the velocity of detected objects is known as ___.
___ is observed when a star and the earth are moving away from each other.
Doppler ultrasound technique used to examine organ such as heart is called ___.
The pitch of a note is determined by its ___.
The loudness of a note depends upon the ___ of the wave.
The characteristic which distinguishes two sounds of the same loudness and same pitch is ___.
The timbre of a sound depends on its ___, which varies with overtones.
Intensity is directly proportional to the ___ of the amplitude of the wave.
___ is a sensation and is subjective to the human ear.
